etymology.description: 没(沒) (méi, "not") and 有(有) (yǒu, "have") together express the concept of "not have" or "there is not" by directly negating the notion of possessing or existing.
etymology.interpretation: 沒 (méi) negates the presence or possession suggested by 有 (yǒu, "have"). This combination intuitively depicts the absence or non-existence of something that one might otherwise possess or expect to exist.

examples.chinese: 今天没有会议(今天沒有會議)。
他没有看到那个信号(他沒有看到那個信號)。
examples.english: He did not see that signal.

examples.chinese: 我们没有太多时间(我們沒有太多時間)。
